metadata.description: Letter from James Nance to Mary Butler Fair. He reports a measles outbreak in camp. He also reports that "Robert has returned to us once more…He has not asked me regularly to enroll him yet. Drayt delivered your message—that we must send him home; and what ever your wishes are, so far as his joining the company again—I am disposed to be guided by." He says that he will also attempt to guide Billy in the way she would prefer [remain at school].
metadata.title: 1861-08-14; Letter from James D. Nance (near Vienna, Virginia) to Mary Butler Fair [Newberry, S.C.] - Page 1r - William Drayton Rutherford Papers, 1840–1897 - UofSC Digital Collections
